_metadata.purpose: Golden-receipt fixed-input-vector for v0.2 cross-track byte-identity demonstration (cross-track conformance deliverable)
_metadata.spec_source: v0.2 receipt schema; cross-track input-vector specification.

receipt_inputs._note: Fixed scalar field values that are direct literal inputs to the receipt constructor. Hash-typed fields (policy_decision_hash, classified_intent_hash, execution_result_hash, artifact_digest, attestation_digest) are derived deterministically per _sentinel_seeds rules below; their resolved wire-form values are persisted alongside rust-golden.json. signature and receipt_hash are derived at sign-step / hash-step per _construction_procedure below.

_sentinel_seeds._note: Each hash-typed receipt input is derived deterministically by SHA-256 over the UTF-8 byte sequence of the documented seed string. This rule MUST produce byte-identical results across all three tracks (rust, python, typescript). Cross-track parity testing begins by independently re-deriving these values and comparing to the rust-golden.json source of truth.

_serialize_skip_optional_fields._note: Forward-compat optional fields are OMITTED from baseline. They MUST NOT appear in canonical wire bytes due to #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]. Every track MUST honor this same skip-when-None rule.

_explicit_null_fields._note: Fields that MUST serialize as explicit JSON null (the nullable-mandatory wire-format invariant), not be skipped. Verified by a unit test in the reference Rust implementation.
_explicit_null_fields.receipt_id_when_none: would emit explicit `null` — baseline uses a populated value, so this rule is not exercised by this baseline
_explicit_null_fields.prev_hash_when_none: MUST emit explicit `null` — baseline IS chain-genesis (prev_hash = None), so this rule IS exercised

_construction_procedure._note: S1 clear-defaults procedure. Each track follows this exact sequence to compute signature and receipt_hash.
_construction_procedure.step_1_construct: Build the receipt struct: load all literal fields from receipt_inputs above; derive sentinel-hash fields per _sentinel_seeds; set signature = "" (empty-string sentinel); set receipt_hash = 32 zero bytes; prev_hash = None.
_construction_procedure.step_2_canonicalize_for_hash: Serialize per RFC 8785 (sorted keys, no whitespace, deterministic). Omit serialize-skip optional fields entirely. Emit explicit `null` for prev_hash. The resulting canonical bytes are the input to the receipt-hash compute step.
_construction_procedure.step_3_compute_receipt_hash: receipt_hash = SHA-256(canonical_bytes_from_step_2). Stored on the receipt struct as a raw 32-byte hash; wire-form bare 64-hex lowercase.
_construction_procedure.step_4_compute_signature: signature = Ed25519_sign(signing_key, canonical_bytes_from_step_2). NOTE: the message-bytes signed are the canonical bytes WITH signature="" AND receipt_hash=zero (i.e. the same bytes as step 2/3 input, not the post-hash bytes). Wire-form base64 standard-padded matching ^[A-Za-z0-9+/]{86}==$.
_construction_procedure.step_5_finalize_for_wire: Populate signature and receipt_hash on the receipt struct; re-canonicalize per RFC 8785. The resulting bytes are the final canonical wire-form receipt and the source-of-truth for cross-track byte-identity comparison. Persisted to rust-golden.json (pretty-printed JSON view) and the canonical-bytes blob to rust-golden.canonical (raw bytes).
_construction_procedure.step_6_optional_verify: Independently re-derive: re-canonicalize the final receipt with signature="" and receipt_hash=32 zero bytes; assert SHA-256 equals the stored receipt_hash; assert Ed25519_verify(public_key, those_canonical_bytes, signature) returns true.

_invariant_axes_under_test.rfc_8785_canonicalization: Byte-identical JSON canonicalization across tracks (sorted keys, no whitespace, escape rules per RFC 8785).
_invariant_axes_under_test.skip_when_none_for_serialize_skip_option: #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] semantics for sigstore_signature, classifier_version, shadow_mode (forward-compat optionals) — MUST NOT emit the key.
_invariant_axes_under_test.explicit_null_for_prev_hash: prev_hash = None MUST emit explicit `null`, not be skipped.
_invariant_axes_under_test.explicit_null_for_receipt_id_when_none: receipt_id = None MUST emit explicit `null` (nullable-mandatory). Not exercised by this baseline (uses a populated value); follow-on extension fixture suggested.
_invariant_axes_under_test.sha256_hash_wire_form: Bare-hash fields (policy_decision_hash, classified_intent_hash, execution_result_hash, receipt_hash) MUST be bare 64-hex lowercase. Prefixed-digest fields (artifact_digest, attestation_digest) AND prev_hash MUST carry the literal `"sha256:"` prefix.
_invariant_axes_under_test.git_commit_wire_form: git_commit MUST be bare 40-character lowercase SHA-1 hex (regex ^[a-f0-9]{40}$). Baseline uses 40 zero characters.
_invariant_axes_under_test.artifact_type_wire_form: artifact_type is a closed taxonomy; the generic-binary variant serializes as the literal kebab-case string `"generic-binary"` (NOT `"generic_binary"`). In the reference Rust implementation this is `#[serde(rename = "generic-binary")]` on the enum variant. outcome (WitnessOutcome) uses snake_case via `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]` — distinct from artifact_type's kebab-case rename; tracks MUST emit each enum by its own scheme.
_invariant_axes_under_test.finalized_at_precision: Baseline uses `"2026-05-26T10:00:00.000Z"` — RFC 3339 UTC with millisecond `.000Z` suffix. Tracks normalizing to different precision MUST surface the divergence.
